description:
Goal: bring compiler2's parser output in-house so `IndexCode` no longer stores shared `src/ast::Item` trees as its source authority.

Signal:
- `IndexCode` records compiler2-owned quoted/source AST for top-level code
- `ModuleSourceKind` and code/module source records no longer depend on shared `Rc<Item>` as the canonical compiler2 surface
- parser tests cover Elixir-shaped source forms needed by the mini-epic: function/macro definitions, modules, attributes, alias/import/require, protocol forms, quote/unquote, item calls, pipe/headless case, capture syntax, maps/structs/bitstrings, and patterns
- legacy parser/AST remains legacy-owned; compiler2 does not ask the old frontend to pre-expand or desugar source

Change:
- add a compiler2 parser/front-door module that emits the owned source AST from `.11.7.1`
- add first-class `require` syntax or equivalent source form support at this boundary
- move compiler2 source discovery (`discover_modules`, protocol/module indexing) over the owned AST
- decide by inspection whether the existing lexer is neutral enough to reuse; fork lexer behavior too if tokenization embeds old AST policy

Why:
- quoted source production cannot be correct-by-construction if the old parser tree remains the hidden authority
- `require` and source-order macro availability are parser/source facts, not downstream lowering concerns
title: Compiler2 parser front door: parse directly into owned source AST
